# services.invidious

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.invidious`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.invidious.address` | `string` | The IP address Invidious should bind to. |
| `services.invidious.database.createLocally` | `boolean` | Whether to create a local database with PostgreSQL. |
| `services.invidious.database.host` | `null or string` | The database host Invidious should use. If `null`, the local unix socket is used. Otherwise TCP is used. |
| `services.invidious.database.passwordFile` | `null or string` | Path to file containing the database password. |
| `services.invidious.database.port` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | The port of the database Invidious should use. Defaults to the the default postgresql port. |
| `services.invidious.domain` | `null or string` | The FQDN Invidious is reachable on. This is used to configure nginx and for building absolute URLs. |
| `services.invidious.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Invidious. |
| `services.invidious.extraSettingsFile` | `null or string` | A file including Invidious settings. It gets merged with the settings specified in {option}`services.invidious.settings` and can be used to store secrets like `hmac_key` outside of the nix store. |
| `services.invidious.hmacKeyFile` | `null or absolute path` | A path to a file containing the `hmac_key`. If `null`, a key will be generated automatically on first start. If non-`null`, this option overrides any `hmac_key` specified in {option}`services.invidious.settings` or via {option}`services.invidious.extraSettingsFile`. |
| `services.invidious.http3-ytproxy.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable http3-ytproxy for faster loading of images and video playback. If {option}`services.invidious.nginx.enable` is used, nginx will be configured automatically. If not, you need to configure a reverse proxy yourself according to <https://docs.invidious.io/improve-public-instance/#3-speed-up-video-playback-with-http3-ytproxy>. |
| `services.invidious.http3-ytproxy.package` | `package` | The http3-ytproxy package to use. |
| `services.invidious.nginx.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to configure nginx as a reverse proxy for Invidious. It serves it under the domain specified in {option}`services.invidious.settings.domain` with enabled TLS and ACME. Further configuration can be done through {option}`services.nginx.virtualHosts.${config.services.invidious.settings.domain}.*`, which can also be used to disable AMCE and TLS. |
| `services.invidious.package` | `package` | The invidious package to use. |
| `services.invidious.port` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | The port Invidious should listen on. To allow access from outside, you can use either {option}`services.invidious.nginx` or add `config.services.invidious.port` to {option}`networking.firewall.allowedTCPPorts`. |
| `services.invidious.serviceScale` | `signed integer` | How many invidious instances to run. See <https://docs.invidious.io/improve-public-instance/#2-multiple-invidious-processes> for more details on how this is intended to work. All instances beyond the first one have the options `channel_threads` and `feed_threads` set to 0 to avoid conflicts with multiple instances refreshing subscriptions. Instances will be configured to bind to consecutive ports starting with {option}`services.invidious.port` for the first instance. |
| `services.invidious.settings` | `JSON value` | The settings Invidious should use. See [config.example.yml](https://github.com/iv-org/invidious/blob/master/config/config.example.yml) for a list of all possible options. |
| `services.invidious.sig-helper.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable and configure inv-sig-helper to emulate the youtube client's javascript. This is required to make certain videos playable. This will download and run completely untrusted javascript from youtube! While this service is sandboxed, this may still be an issue! |
| `services.invidious.sig-helper.listenAddress` | `string` | The IP address/port where inv-sig-helper should listen. |
| `services.invidious.sig-helper.package` | `package` | The inv-sig-helper package to use. |
